title: Polypodium beddomei
taxonomy: Polypodium beddomei is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns. Further, it is placed in the class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Polypodiaceae, and genus Polypodium. This classification indicates its position as a species of fern within the larger plant kingdom. The family Polypodiaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as polypod ferns or strap ferns. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Polypodium beddomei.
uses: Polypodium beddomei is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Its leaves are also used to treat gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea and dysentery. The plant is reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial in reducing swelling and pain. Polypodium beddomei is also used to treat respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
